generated_oml:
// C2: Acting Components
instance HVACControllerActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Modular actuator component for HVAC system control defined within BIM environment, enabling automated adjustments to dampers, valves, and fans for energy efficiency and occupant comfort optimization"
]

instance AutomatedShadingActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "Service-oriented actuator for automated window shading systems integrated through standardized extension points, supporting dynamic adjustment based on environmental conditions and occupancy patterns"
]

instance LightingControlActuator : DTDFVocab:ActingComponent [
    base:desc "BIM-centric lighting control actuator enabling remote configuration and deployment of LED panels or automated luminaires for adaptive environmental management"
]

// C3: Physical Sensing Components
instance OccupantBehaviorSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "IoT-based sensor capturing real-time occupant movement patterns and behavior analytics for building performance assessment"
    DTDFVocab:producedData OccupantBehaviorDataTransmitted
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "High-precision IoT sensor measuring indoor temperature with real-time synchronization capabilities for thermal comfort monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted
]

instance RelativeHumiditySensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Indoor humidity sensor providing continuous monitoring of relative humidity levels for environmental condition optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData RelativeHumidityDataTransmitted
]

instance CarbonDioxideSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Air quality sensor measuring CO₂ concentrations to support ventilation system optimization and occupant health monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:producedData CarbonDioxideDataTransmitted
]

instance LightingLevelSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Ambient light sensor capturing real-time lighting conditions to enable adaptive lighting control strategies"
    DTDFVocab:producedData LightingLevelDataTransmitted
]

instance HVACEnergyConsumptionSensor : DTDFVocab:SensingComponent [
    base:desc "Energy monitoring sensor tracking HVAC system power consumption for performance analysis and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:producedData HVACEnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
]

// C4: Physical-to-Virtual Interaction
instance OccupantBehaviorDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom OccupantBehaviorSensor
]

instance EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom EnvironmentalTemperatureSensor
]

instance RelativeHumidityDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom RelativeHumiditySensor
]

instance CarbonDioxideDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom CarbonDioxideSensor
]

instance LightingLevelDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom LightingLevelSensor
]

instance HVACEnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted : DTDFVocab:DataTransmitted [
    DTDFVocab:producedFrom HVACEnergyConsumptionSensor
]

// C10: Models/Data
instance BIMEnrichedModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Semantically enriched BIM model incorporating Building Control System components (sensors, actuators) with automated validation checks for semantic consistency"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo EclipseHonoEnabler, EclipseDittoEnabler
]

instance OperationalDigitalTwinModel : DTDFVocab:Model [
    base:desc "Runtime operational model generated from enriched BIM through model transformation pipelines, supporting real-time synchronization with physical assets"
    DTDFVocab:inputTo UnrealEngineVisualizationEnabler, PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance OccupantBehaviorData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Structured representation of real-time occupant movement patterns and behavior analytics for performance assessment"
    DTDFVocab:fromData OccupantBehaviorDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler
]

instance EnvironmentalParametersData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Aggregated environmental data including temperature, humidity, and CO₂ levels for indoor condition monitoring"
    DTDFVocab:fromData EnvironmentalTemperatureDataTransmitted, RelativeHumidityDataTransmitted, CarbonDioxideDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance EnergyConsumptionData : DTDFVocab:Data [
    base:desc "Time-series energy consumption data from HVAC and lighting systems for performance benchmarking and optimization"
    DTDFVocab:fromData HVACEnergyConsumptionDataTransmitted
    DTDFVocab:inputTo DataDrivenOptimizationService
]

// C11: Enablers
instance EclipseHonoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Hono IoT platform enabling real-time data ingestion and device management for bidirectional physical-to-virtual synchronization"
    DTDFVocab:enables RealTimeMonitoringService, DataDrivenOptimizationService
]

instance EclipseDittoEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Eclipse Ditto digital twin platform providing centralized twin management and standardized extension points for service integration"
    DTDFVocab:enables ClosedLoopControlService, InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance UnrealEngineVisualizationEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Unreal Engine 5-based visualization engine providing immersive 3D representations for interactive monitoring and scenario simulation"
    DTDFVocab:enables InteractiveVisualizationService
]

instance PredictiveAnalyticsEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Custom service plugin framework enabling deployment of predictive models for energy consumption forecasting and equipment failure prediction"
    DTDFVocab:enables PredictiveMaintenanceService
]

instance ModelDrivenEngineeringEnabler : DTDFVocab:Enabler [
    base:desc "Model-Driven Engineering framework automating twin generation from BIM models through structured representation and transformation pipelines"
    DTDFVocab:enables BIMEnrichedModel
]

// C6: Services
instance RealTimeMonitoringService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Real-time tracking service for occupant behavior, energy consumption, and environmental parameters with continuous synchronization capabilities"
    DTDFVocab:provides OccupantBehaviorInsight, EnvironmentalConditionInsight
]

instance DataDrivenOptimizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive optimization service adjusting HVAC and lighting systems for energy efficiency and occupant comfort through data-driven control strategies"
    DTDFVocab:provides EnergyOptimizationAction, ComfortOptimizationAction
]

instance InteractiveVisualizationService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D dashboard service enabling stakeholders to analyze operational data and validate control strategies through interactive visualization"
    DTDFVocab:provides SystemStateVisualizationInsight, ScenarioSimulationAction
]

instance ClosedLoopControlService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Automated control service integrating with Building Automation Systems to enable real-time feedback loops between digital twin and physical systems"
    DTDFVocab:provides HVACControlAction, ShadingControlAction
]

instance PredictiveMaintenanceService : DTDFVocab:Service [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics service identifying potential equipment failures through real-time data analysis and confidence-aware recommendations"
    DTDFVocab:provides EquipmentHealthInsight, MaintenanceRecommendationAction
]

// C17: Insights/Actions
instance OccupantBehaviorInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Real-time analytics on occupant movement patterns and behavior for building performance assessment and operational optimization"
]

instance EnvironmentalConditionInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Continuous monitoring of indoor environmental parameters (temperature, humidity, CO₂) to detect deviations from optimal conditions"
]

instance EnergyOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated adjustments to HVAC and lighting systems based on predictive analytics to achieve energy efficiency targets"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ComfortOptimizationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic environmental control adjustments maintaining predefined thermal comfort thresholds through real-time sensor feedback"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance SystemStateVisualizationInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Immersive 3D visualization of current building system states enabling stakeholders to observe operational conditions and detect anomalies"
]

instance ScenarioSimulationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Interactive simulation capability allowing testing of different operational scenarios within the digital twin environment"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

instance HVACControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Automated control of HVAC dampers, valves, and fans through bidirectional communication with physical systems"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance ShadingControlAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Dynamic adjustment of automated window shading systems based on environmental conditions and occupancy patterns"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic true
]

instance EquipmentHealthInsight : DTDFVocab:Insight [
    base:desc "Predictive analytics output identifying potential equipment failures with confidence intervals for proactive maintenance planning"
]

instance MaintenanceRecommendationAction : DTDFVocab:Action [
    base:desc "Confidence-aware maintenance suggestions presented to facility managers for equipment health optimization"
    DTDFVocab:IsAutomatic false
]

// C1: Environments/Components
instance AcademicBuildingEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "LEED Platinum certified academic building serving as the physical twin for indoor environmental monitoring and control"
    base:contains HVACSystemComponent, LightingSystemComponent, OccupantZoneComponent
]

instance LibraryEnvironment : DTDFVocab:Environment [
    base:desc "University library environment with specialized indoor conditions for reading and research activities"
    base:contains HVACSystemComponent, AutomatedShadingComponent, OccupantZoneComponent
]

instance AcademicBuildingSystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Academic building system under study for digital twin implementation"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance LibrarySystem : DTDFVocab:SystemUnderStudy [
    base:desc "Library system under study for digital twin implementation"
    DTDFVocab:hasEnvironment LibraryEnvironment
]

instance HVACSystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Building Control System component including HVAC units, dampers, and valves for environmental temperature and humidity control"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment, LibraryEnvironment
]

instance LightingSystemComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Automated lighting infrastructure including LED panels and sensors for adaptive lighting control"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment
]

instance AutomatedShadingComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Window shading system with motorized blinds for solar heat gain control and daylight harvesting"
    base:isContainedIn LibraryEnvironment
]

instance OccupantZoneComponent : DTDFVocab:Component [
    base:desc "Defined spatial zones within buildings capturing occupant density and behavior patterns for environmental management"
    base:isContainedIn AcademicBuildingEnvironment, LibraryEnvironment
]

instance virtual_to_physical_interaction : DTDFVocab:VirtualToPhysical [
    base:desc "The **virtual-to-physical interaction** in this Digital Twin system is facilitated through **interactive visualization** and **runtime control interfaces**, enabling facility managers and building control engineers to monitor and adjust physical systems in real time. The framework supports **bidirectional command flows** via the central DT hub, allowing for **control actions** (e.g., HVAC adjustments, lighting management) to be executed on physical infrastructure. While the **command types** (e.g., emergency response protocols) and **control message formats** (e.g., OPC UA service calls) are not explicitly specified, the system’s **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points** imply support for **custom control logic**. **Validation and authentication mechanisms**, **feedback systems**, and **conflict resolution** are not detailed in the documents, though the framework’s **runtime configuration management** suggests adaptability to dynamic operational needs."
]

instance twinning_time_scale : DTDFVocab:TimeScale [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system supports **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital counterparts, ensuring continuous synchronization without explicitly defined sub-second or millisecond intervals. The framework emphasizes **dynamic operational needs** with **low-latency requirements** implied by the term 'real-time,' though no specific latency thresholds (e.g., <100ms, <1s) are quantified. Temporal granularity is aligned with operational monitoring and control workflows, enabling **bidirectional, near-instantaneous updates** for Facility Managers and Building Control Engineers. The system does not explicitly mention time scales for individual services beyond the overarching real-time synchronization requirement, nor does it detail latency requirements for specific use cases (e.g., predictive maintenance, energy optimization)."
]

instance dt_constellation : DTDFVocab:Constellation [
    base:desc "The Digital Twin system follows a **centralized-decentralized hybrid orchestration pattern**, where the *DT Framework* acts as the **core hub** for coordinating roles (Building Control Engineers, Facility Managers, DT Engineers) and system components. Orchestration is achieved through **bidirectional data exchange** between physical assets and digital models, enabled by **modular architecture** with standardized extension points. Resource management is implied via **runtime configuration management**, allowing **adaptive adjustments** without full redeployment, thus optimizing system-wide coordination. The framework does not specify explicit **orchestration protocols** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT, or custom APIs) but emphasizes **interoperability across heterogeneous systems** and **scalability** to entire infrastructure portfolios. Component integration is facilitated by **service-oriented design**, where custom analytics and extensions are added via modular plugins, ensuring loose coupling and high reusability."
]

instance life_cycle_stages : DTDFVocab:EvolutionStage [
    base:desc "
The Digital Twin (DT) lifecycle in this framework spans **five core phases**, each governed by distinct roles and technical processes to address BIM’s static limitations and enable dynamic DT operations. The lifecycle begins with **Device Modeling**, where Building Control Engineers define **sensors, actuators, and controllers** directly within the BIM environment, creating a **unified semantic foundation** for subsequent transformations. This phase leverages **enriched BIM models** to overcome BIM’s inherent static constraints, ensuring semantic interoperability between physical and digital components.

The second phase, **Model-Driven Deployment (MDE)**, involves **automated extraction** of structured representations from enriched BIM models via **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE) principles**. This generates **deployable DT artifacts** with minimal manual intervention, facilitating seamless transition from static BIM representations to dynamic DT components. The third phase, **Runtime Synchronization**, ensures **real-time data exchange** between physical buildings and their digital twins, maintaining consistency through continuous bidirectional communication.

The fourth phase, **Custom Service Integration**, is driven by DT Engineers who extend DT functionality via **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**. This phase emphasizes **plug-and-play custom services** (e.g., analytics, predictive maintenance) to adapt to evolving operational requirements. The final phase, **Facility Operations**, involves **Facility Managers** monitoring and controlling physical infrastructure through immersive **interactive visualizations**, enabling real-time decision-making. **Lifecycle governance** is achieved through **modular architecture**, **standardized interfaces**, and **role-based access control**, ensuring traceability, versioning, and compliance across all phases. Transition management between phases is facilitated by **automated workflows** and **semantic mappings** derived from the initial BIM enrichment process.
"
]

instance fidelity_and_validity_considerations : DTDFVocab:FidelityConsideration [
    base:desc "Fidelity is ensured through **semantic integration** of BIM models with runtime data, closing the gap between design-time and operational models. The system employs **Design Science Research (DSR)** methodology to validate the Digital Twin’s effectiveness, with **Overall Digital Twin Entanglement (ODTE)** as the primary metric for evaluating model fidelity. Uncertainty sources are mitigated via real-time synchronization, while error detection and correction mechanisms are embedded in the **modular, service-oriented architecture**. Quality assurance processes are implicitly tied to the **reproducible deployment framework**, ensuring consistency across deployments and adherence to AECO domain standards."
]

instance dt_technical_connection : DTDFVocab:TechnicalConnection [
    base:desc "The **technical network infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is characterized by a **modular architecture** with **standardized extension points**, enabling interoperability across heterogeneous systems. The framework relies on **Eclipse Hono and Ditto** for connectivity, though the underlying **communication protocols** (e.g., MQTT, CoAP, LON, Modbus) and **network topology** (e.g., distributed, hybrid) are not explicitly described. The system prioritizes **interoperability**, **scalability**, and **low-latency communication** to support real-time interactions. **Reliability and redundancy mechanisms** are implied through the framework’s **runtime configuration management**, allowing adjustments without full redeployment. However, **bandwidth requirements**, **latency specifications**, and **security protocols** (e.g., TLS, encryption) are not detailed in the provided documents."
]

instance dt_hosting_deployment : DTDFVocab:Deployment [
    base:desc "The **deployment infrastructure** for this Digital Twin system is designed with a **modular architecture** and **runtime configuration management**, enabling **low-overhead updates** and **scalability** from individual components to entire infrastructure systems. The framework leverages **cloud components** (e.g., Eclipse Ditto) and **visualization tools** (e.g., Unreal Engine) for deployment, though explicit support for **edge deployment** or **on-premise deployment** is not mentioned. **Computational and storage requirements** are implied to accommodate dynamic operational needs, and the system emphasizes **availability and reliability** through adaptable configurations. However, specific **deployment models** (e.g., Kubernetes-based orchestration), **scalability mechanisms**, or **availability assurances** (e.g., SLAs) are not detailed in the documents."
]

instance horizontal_integration : DTDFVocab:HoriIntegration [
    base:desc "Horizontal integration is addressed through **non-functional requirements (NF_X)** focusing on **interoperability across heterogeneous systems**, leveraging standardized extension points and **modular architecture** to facilitate seamless data exchange. The framework supports integration with external systems via **industry-standard protocols** (implicitly assumed, though not explicitly named) and adheres to **AECO domain conventions** for BIM-to-DT transitions. Dependencies on external systems are managed through **runtime configuration**, enabling dynamic adjustments without redeployment. Integration patterns are not detailed beyond the **centralized hub’s role** in coordinating interactions with BIM tools, facility management systems, and custom services. The system does not explicitly mention **enterprise integration patterns** (e.g., SOA, microservices) or **Digital Twin constellations** (e.g., federated twins) but prioritizes **scalability** and **low-overhead updates** to support heterogeneous ecosystems."
]

instance standardization : DTDFVocab:Standardization [
    base:desc "
The framework adheres to **BIM standards** as its foundational data model, leveraging enriched BIM representations to address limitations in static building information. **Model-Driven Engineering (MDE)** principles govern the transformation pipeline, ensuring standardized artifact generation from BIM models. While specific **industry standards** (e.g., IFC, COBie) or **certification frameworks** (e.g., ISO 23247) are not mentioned, the framework implicitly aligns with **BIM interoperability requirements** and **MDE compliance** as outlined in research projects (TwinLight, BOREALIS).

Interoperability is supported by **modular architecture** and **standardized extension points**, enabling seamless integration of custom services. However, **protocol-level standards** (e.g., OPC UA, MQTT) or **compliance frameworks** (e.g., GDPR) are not referenced in the provided documents. The framework’s adherence to **BIM and MDE** is the primary standardization anchor, with no explicit mention of additional compliance or certification requirements.
"
]
